figures.caption: Table 4: Example audio–text retrieval results. “Good” indicates successful retrieval where the model correctly identifies the queried event, while “Bad” indicates retrieval failures.
Table 5: Ablation studies with different audio and text encoders, different projection types, and different loss functions on the Clotho dataset.

Table 8: Examples of audio captions generated by LTU and Gemini 2.5 Pro. The column labeled groundtruth contains reference human-written captions. The discrepancies between model outputs and ground truth highlight the challenges of accurate audio caption generation.
Table 9: Evaluation results of different models and retrieval methods on the AudioCaps dataset using caption generation followed by text based search.

Table 1: Audio–text retrieval results across datasets and models. The Modality column indicates audio-to-text (a2t) or text-to-audio (t2a) retrieval. Metrics include Recall@K (K = 1, 5, 10) and mAP@10 (higher is better).
Table 3: Text to audio retrieval on noisy datasets with different levels of noise (SNR ranges from 5 to 15).
Table 2: Audio to text retrieval on datasets with different levels (SNR ranges from 5 to 15) of noise.
